title: Safety and Immunogenicity of a Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 Spike Subunit Vaccine Stabilized in the Prefusion Conformation by a Second-Generation Molecular Clamp and Evaluated in Adults Aged 18-55 Years: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Active Comparator, Phase I Trial.

why_it_matters_points: Ongoing development of COVID-19 vaccines is crucial for addressing emerging variants and improving vaccine platforms.
Previous molecular clamp vaccine designs faced issues with HIV diagnostic interference, necessitating the development of safer alternatives.
A new molecular clamp technology could enable rapid and versatile vaccine development against various respiratory viruses, enhancing pandemic preparedness.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: The UQSC2 vaccine, utilizing a second-generation molecular clamp, was as well tolerated as the NVX-CoV2373 comparator and elicited comparable increases in neutralizing humoral responses against the prototypic Wuhan strain of SARS-CoV-2.
clinical_takeaway: This Phase 1 data suggests the MC2 platform is safe and immunogenic, but further larger trials are needed to confirm efficacy and broader variant coverage.
The primary limitation is the small sample size and focus on a single booster dose in previously vaccinated individuals, limiting generalizability to primary vaccination or other populations.
Watch for future studies on this platform, as it could offer a versatile approach for rapid vaccine development against novel pathogens.

title: RSV Vaccination in Solid Organ Transplant Recipients: Interim Findings From a Phase 3 Trial of mRNA-1345.

why_it_matters_points: Solid organ transplant recipients are highly vulnerable to severe respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) disease due to their chronic immunosuppression.
Current RSV prevention strategies are limited for this population, creating a significant unmet medical need.
A safe and effective RSV vaccine for transplant recipients could substantially reduce morbidity and mortality in this high-risk group.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: The mRNA-1345 RSV vaccine was well tolerated and immunogenic in solid organ transplant recipients, with a single dose leading to 4.9-fold and 3.4-fold increases in RSV-A and RSV-B neutralizing antibody GMTs by Day 29, respectively.
clinical_takeaway: Consider the mRNA-1345 vaccine for solid organ transplant recipients once approved, as it appears safe and immunogenic in this vulnerable population.

A single dose provides robust antibody responses, but a second dose may offer additional benefit, particularly for kidney and lung transplant recipients, those <2 years post-transplant, or those on mycophenolate.
This is an interim analysis of an ongoing open-label Phase 3 trial, so long-term efficacy and safety data are still pending.

title: Safety and Immunogenicity of a Klebsiella pneumoniae Tetravalent Bioconjugate Vaccine (Kleb4V) Administered to Healthy Adults: A First-in-Human Phase I/II Randomized and Controlled Study.

why_it_matters_points: Klebsiella pneumoniae is a major cause of healthcare-associated infections, particularly in vulnerable populations, and is increasingly resistant to antibiotics.
There is an urgent global need for effective preventative strategies, including vaccines, to combat the rising burden of K. pneumoniae infections.
This study represents a critical early step in the development of a K. pneumoniae vaccine, potentially offering a new tool for infection control if successful in later phases.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: The tetravalent bioconjugate vaccine Kleb4V was immunogenic and well tolerated in healthy adults aged 55-70 years, with AS03-adjuvanted formulations showing superior immune responses for three of four serotypes.
clinical_takeaway: This Phase I/II data suggests Kleb4V is a promising vaccine candidate for K. pneumoniae, particularly in older adults, but efficacy data is still needed.
The superior immune response with AS03 adjuvant highlights its potential benefit, though further studies are required to confirm its role in clinical protection.
Responses for the O3b serotype were reduced compared to others, indicating a potential area for vaccine optimization in future development.

title: Cabotegravir plus Rilpivirine for Persons with HIV and Adherence Challenges.

why_it_matters_points: Adherence to daily oral antiretroviral therapy (ART) remains a significant challenge for many persons with HIV, leading to virologic failure and drug resistance.
Long-acting injectable ART offers a potential solution to adherence barriers, but randomized trial evidence in populations with documented adherence challenges has been lacking.
This study provides crucial evidence supporting the use of long-acting injectables in a high-need population, potentially transforming care for those struggling with oral ART.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Monthly injections of long-acting cabotegravir-rilpivirine significantly reduced the cumulative incidence of regimen failure by week 48 to 22.8% compared to 41.2% with standard oral ART (difference, -18.4 percentage points; P = 0.002) in persons with HIV and adherence challenges.
clinical_takeaway: Consider long-acting injectable cabotegravir-rilpivirine for persons with HIV who have documented adherence challenges to oral ART and achieve virologic suppression with support.
While effective, careful monitoring for virologic failure and resistance is still necessary, as resistance-associated mutations developed in a small number of participants in both groups.
The study population received adherence support and incentives prior to randomization, suggesting that a period of stabilization may be beneficial before initiating injectables.

title: Immune Responses to BK Virus in Renal Transplant Recipients Receiving Intravenous Immunoglobulin Treatment.

why_it_matters_points: BK polyomavirus DNAemia is a common and serious complication in renal transplant recipients, increasing the risk of allograft failure.
Specific antiviral therapies for BKPyV are currently unavailable, leading to reliance on immunosuppression reduction, which carries its own risks.
Investigating host immune responses and potential adjunctive treatments like IVIG is critical for improving outcomes in this vulnerable patient population.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: In this pilot study, 40% of IVIG recipients and 44.4% of placebo recipients cleared BK DNAemia at 3 months (relative risk, 0.90 [95% CI, .23-2.89]), indicating no clear impact of IVIG on viral clearance.
clinical_takeaway: Two doses of IVIG (1 g/kg) for BK DNAemia in renal transplant recipients appear safe but did not demonstrate a clear benefit in viral clearance in this pilot study.
Viral clearance may be more dependent on intrinsic virus-specific host cellular and humoral responses, suggesting a need for further research into these mechanisms.
Given the lack of efficacy, IVIG should not be routinely used for BK DNAemia based on these findings, and immunosuppression reduction remains the primary strategy.

title: "Should I stay or should I go"-a kinase delays escape of Candida glabrata from macrophages.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

why_it_matters_points: Candida glabrata is a major cause of invasive candidiasis, accounting for 15%-25% of cases, and is often difficult to treat due to intrinsic antifungal resistance.
The ability of C. glabrata to survive and proliferate within host macrophages contributes to immune evasion and persistent infections.
Understanding the molecular mechanisms governing C. glabrata's intracellular lifestyle is crucial for developing novel diagnostic and therapeutic strategies against this pathogen.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Candida glabrata actively delays its escape from macrophages, a process regulated by the Ksp1 kinase, with deletion of KSP1 accelerating macrophage lysis and increasing resistance to antifungals.
clinical_takeaway: C. glabrata may actively prolong its intramacrophage phase, potentially contributing to immune evasion, antifungal resistance, and recurrent infections.

The identification of the Ksp1 kinase and its role in mitochondrial dysfunction and the formation of respiration-deficient variants (petites) highlights a potential target for novel antifungal strategies.

Clinicians should be aware that C. glabrata's intracellular survival and the generation of stress-resistant variants may necessitate adapted clinical diagnostics and therapy for persistent and recurrent fungal infections.

title: Comparative genomic, phenotypic, and clinical characterization of ST11-KL25 and ST11-KL64 hypervirulent carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae in a tertiary hospital, 2020-2023.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

why_it_matters_points: Hypervirulent carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae (hv-CRKP) poses a significant challenge due to its combined virulence and multidrug resistance.
ST11 is the predominant lineage of hv-CRKP in China, with KL64 and the recently identified KL25 as key capsular subtypes.
Understanding the distinct clinical and genomic characteristics of these subtypes is crucial for effective surveillance and intervention strategies.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: ST11-KL64 exhibited broader drug resistance, including higher rates of ceftazidime-avibactam and polymyxin resistance, while ST11-KL25 showed greater competitive fitness and biofilm formation.
clinical_takeaway: Be aware that ST11-KL64 hv-CRKP may present with broader resistance profiles, including to ceftazidime-avibactam and polymyxin, compared to ST11-KL25.
Consider subtype-specific surveillance in regions where ST11-KL25 and ST11-KL64 are prevalent, as their distinct adaptation strategies may require tailored infection control.
This study is limited to a single tertiary hospital in China, and findings may not be generalizable to other geographic regions or healthcare settings.

title: CRISPR-Cas-associated SCCmec Variants in Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus Evade Rapid Diagnostic Detection.

why_it_matters_points: Rapid molecular assays are critical for timely diagnosis and appropriate treatment of methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) infections.
False-negative results from these assays can lead to delayed or incorrect antibiotic therapy, potentially worsening patient outcomes.
Understanding novel mechanisms of diagnostic evasion is crucial for maintaining the accuracy and reliability of rapid diagnostic tools.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: CRISPR-Cas-associated SCCmec variants, found in 2% of 2432 screened S. aureus isolates, caused misdiagnosis in 11 of 40 SCCmec/junctions by BCID2 or Xpert assays, primarily restricted to clonal complex 5.
clinical_takeaway: Be aware that rapid molecular MRSA diagnostic assays (e.g., Xpert, BCID2) can yield false-negative results due to novel SCCmec variants, particularly in hospital-associated clonal complex 5.
If clinical suspicion for MRSA remains high despite a negative rapid molecular test, consider conventional culture and susceptibility testing to avoid misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment.
This finding highlights the need for ongoing surveillance of diagnostic assay performance and potential updates to primer designs to ensure continued accuracy.

title: Tenecteplase versus standard medical treatment for basilar artery occlusion within 24 h (TRACE-5): a multicentre, prospective, randomised, open-label, blinded-endpoint, superiority, phase 3 trial.

why_it_matters_points: Basilar artery occlusion (BAO) is a severe form of ischemic stroke associated with high mortality and disability rates, posing a significant clinical challenge.
Current treatment options for BAO, especially beyond the standard 4.5-hour window for alteplase, are limited, often relying on endovascular thrombectomy which may not always be available or feasible.
Expanding the therapeutic window for intravenous thrombolysis could offer a crucial intervention for patients presenting later, potentially improving functional outcomes for a broader population.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Intravenous tenecteplase administered within 24 hours of symptom onset significantly improved functional outcome at 90 days, with 38% of patients achieving an mRS score of 0-1 or return to baseline, compared to 29% in the standard medical treatment group (adjusted relative rate 1.50, 95% CI 1.09-2.08, p=0.014).
clinical_takeaway: Consider tenecteplase (0.25 mg/kg, max 25 mg) for patients with basilar artery occlusion presenting within 24 hours of symptom onset, particularly when endovascular thrombectomy is delayed or unavailable.
While symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage rates were similar, this study was conducted in China, and generalizability to diverse populations and healthcare systems should be considered.
Tenecteplase appears to be a safe and effective option, but its optimal role in conjunction with or as an alternative to endovascular thrombectomy requires further clarification.

title: Ιdentification of SQ109 analogs with enhanced antimicrobial activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus.

why_it_matters_points: The rise of antimicrobial resistance, particularly in methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), necessitates the development of novel antibacterial agents.
SQ109 is a lipid-based antimicrobial candidate, and its analogs could offer new therapeutic avenues against difficult-to-treat infections.
Identifying compounds with potent activity and novel mechanisms against MRSA, including persister cells, is critical for future treatment strategies.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Analogs AK126 and AK127, featuring bulky benzyl- or phenyl-substituents, exhibited the most potent antimicrobial activity against MRSA with no detectable resistance development.
clinical_takeaway: Monitor for further development of SQ109 analogs like AK126 and AK127, as they show promise against MRSA, including persister cells, and synergize with gentamicin.
These findings are from in vitro studies and require extensive in vivo validation and clinical trials before any therapeutic application.
The mechanism of action involves membrane disruption and proton motive force inhibition, which is a promising target for new antimicrobials.

title: Corticosteroid Therapy and Long-Term Outcomes of Post-Infectious Inflammatory Syndrome in Non-HIV Immunosuppressed Cryptococcal Meningitis: A Multicenter Case Series.

why_it_matters_points: Post-infectious inflammatory response syndrome (PIIRS) is a significant cause of neurological deterioration in cryptococcal meningoencephalitis (CM).
Data on PIIRS in non-HIV immunosuppressed patients with CM is limited, making treatment decisions challenging for this vulnerable population.
Effective management strategies are needed to improve long-term neurological outcomes and reduce mortality in these patients.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Corticosteroid therapy was associated with significant improvements in Karnofsky scores at 1 month (P = .001), with sustained benefit at 6 and 12 months (P = .002), and all 10 surviving patients demonstrated resolution of neurological symptoms.
clinical_takeaway: Consider corticosteroids as salvage therapy for non-HIV immunosuppressed patients with PIIRS following CM who experience neurological deterioration.
Be aware of the potential for severe adverse events, as 3 of 13 patients died, including one from sepsis after corticosteroid initiation.
This is a small, multicenter case series; larger, ideally randomized, studies are needed to confirm efficacy, optimal dosing, and safety profile.

title: Trichomonas vaginalis Among Cisgender Women Taking Doxycycline Postexposure Prophylaxis.

why_it_matters_points: Doxycycline postexposure prophylaxis (doxy-PEP) is an important strategy for preventing bacterial sexually transmitted infections (STIs).
Trichomonas vaginalis is a common, curable STI that can cause significant health issues, particularly for cisgender women.
Understanding the full spectrum of doxy-PEP's efficacy against various STIs, including T. vaginalis, is crucial for informing public health guidelines and clinical practice.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Among cisgender women, doxycycline postexposure prophylaxis did not significantly reduce incident T. vaginalis infections compared to standard-of-care (RR: 0.96, 95% CI: 0.54-1.73, P = .9).
clinical_takeaway: Doxy-PEP, as currently prescribed for bacterial STIs, does not appear to offer protection against T. vaginalis infection.
Clinicians should continue to screen for and treat T. vaginalis independently of doxy-PEP use in at-risk populations.
This secondary analysis was conducted in Kenya, and generalizability to other populations or settings should be considered.

title: Phospholipid Transporter MlaFEDCB Regulates Klebsiella pneumoniae Virulence by Modulating Fimbriae Synthesis and Stress-adaptive Growth.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

why_it_matters_points: Klebsiella pneumoniae is a key opportunistic pathogen, with emerging hypervirulent strains posing a growing public health threat.
Elucidating the specific mechanisms of bacterial virulence is essential for identifying novel targets for antimicrobial development.
The MlaFEDCB phospholipid transporter has been linked to bacterial virulence, but its precise regulatory role in K. pneumoniae was previously unclear.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Deletion of the mlaFEDCB gene cluster in Klebsiella pneumoniae significantly prolonged survival in a murine intraperitoneal infection model (P = .0005) by modulating fimbriae synthesis and host cell adherence.
clinical_takeaway: The MlaFEDCB transporter represents a potential novel therapeutic target for reducing Klebsiella pneumoniae virulence by impairing fimbrial adhesion and stress adaptation.
Further research is needed to translate these in vitro and murine model findings into human clinical applications and drug development.

title: Therapeutic effect of eravacycline against carbapenem-resistant hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae in mouse models.

why_it_matters_points: Carbapenem-resistant hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae (CR-hvKP) presents a major clinical challenge due to its combined drug resistance and high virulence.
Effective treatment options for CR-hvKP infections are severely limited, contributing to high morbidity and mortality rates.
There is a critical need to identify and evaluate novel therapeutic agents capable of combating these highly resistant and virulent pathogens.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: In mouse models of CR-hvKP pneumonia, eravacycline treatment significantly reduced bacterial colonization in the lungs (P<0.01), attenuated lung injury and inflammation, and suppressed serum cytokine levels (P<0.05).
clinical_takeaway: Eravacycline demonstrates promising preclinical efficacy against carbapenem-resistant hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae, suggesting its potential as a future therapeutic option.
These findings are from mouse models and require validation through human clinical trials before any clinical recommendations can be made.
Clinicians should be aware of ongoing research into novel agents for CR-hvKP, but current practice remains unchanged.

title: Efimosfermin alfa (BOS-580) once per month in people with metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis with F2 or F3 fibrosis: results from a 24-week, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.

why_it_matters_points: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) is a growing global health concern, often progressing to advanced fibrosis, cirrhosis, and liver failure.
There is a significant unmet need for effective and safe pharmacologic therapies specifically targeting MASH-related fibrosis to prevent disease progression.
Developing once-monthly treatments could improve patient adherence and quality of life compared to more frequent dosing regimens for chronic conditions like MASH.
llm_enrichment.headline_result: Efimosfermin alfa (BOS-580) once per month was generally well tolerated in patients with MASH and F2 or F3 fibrosis over 24 weeks, with no clinically meaningful changes in vital signs or significant grade 3 or higher laboratory abnormalities.
clinical_takeaway: Efimosfermin alfa demonstrates a promising safety and tolerability profile in patients with MASH and moderate to advanced fibrosis, supporting its continued development.

As this was a phase 2 trial primarily focused on safety, efficacy data regarding fibrosis regression or MASH resolution are not the primary outcome and require further investigation in larger phase 3 studies.
Clinicians should monitor for future phase 3 trial results to determine the clinical utility and efficacy of efimosfermin alfa for MASH treatment.

title: Recombinant factor VIIa versus placebo for spontaneous intracerebral haemorrhage within 2 h of symptom onset (FASTEST): a multicentre, double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled, phase 3 trial.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Recombinant factor VIIa for spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage within 2 hours slowed hematoma growth but did not improve functional outcomes and increased thromboembolic events.

title: 2026 AHA/ACC/ACCP/ACEP/CHEST/SCAI/SHM/SIR/SVM/SVN Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Acute Pulmonary Embolism in Adults: A Report of the American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association Joint Committee on Clinical Practice Guidelines.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: New 2026 AHA/ACC guideline provides comprehensive recommendations for acute pulmonary embolism evaluation and management in adults, introducing enhanced severity classification for improved decision-making.

title: Presentation and prognosis of cryptococcosis requiring intensive care unit admission in France: the CRYPTO-ICU study.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Severe cryptococcosis requiring ICU admission increasingly affects non-HIV patients and is associated with high mortality, emphasizing the critical need for early diagnosis and treatment to improve prognosis.

title: In vitro activity of aztreonam-avibactam and distribution of carbapenemase genes in ceftazidime-avibactam-resistant carbapenem-resistant Enterobacterales: data from the global Antimicrobial Testing Leadership and Surveillance, 2019-2023.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Aztreonam-avibactam demonstrates potent in vitro activity against global carbapenem-resistant Enterobacterales, including MBL producers, outperforming other beta-lactam/beta-lactamase inhibitor combinations, but emerging resistance in specific species warrants continued surveillance.

title: Carbapenemase-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae harboring virulence-associated genes among war-affected children from the Gaza Strip.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A letter describing carbapenemase-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae with virulence genes in war-affected children from the Gaza Strip, highlighting a concerning public health issue.

title: An Antibody-Oligonucleotide Conjugate for Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A phase 1-2 trial of delpacibart etedesiran for myotonic dystrophy type 1 showed promising amelioration of aberrant splicing, supporting further investigation despite two serious adverse events.

title: Intrathecal Isoniazid and Dexamethasone Therapy May Improve Outcomes in Patients with Tuberculous Meningitis: A Two-Center Retrospective Cohort Study.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Intrathecal isoniazid and dexamethasone therapy may improve functional outcomes in patients with tuberculous meningitis, suggesting a promising approach to overcome the blood-brain barrier, though further validation is needed.

title: Current infection control practices for multidrug-resistant organisms (MDRO): a survey of the Society for Healthcare Epidemiology of America (SHEA) research network and affiliated US-based hospitals.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A survey reveals significant variation in MDRO infection control practices, highlighting barriers like staff adherence, private room availability, and lack of evidence-based strategies for clearance, indicating a need for better guidance.

title: Development and validation of the AURIS score for predicting candidaemia in Candidozyma auris-colonised patients in the intensive care unit: a bicentric retrospective cohort study.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: The AURIS score, incorporating TPN, prior antifungals, multifocal, and urinary colonization, effectively predicts candidaemia in C. auris-colonized ICU patients, potentially reducing unnecessary empirical antifungal therapy.

title: Withdrawal of aspirin in patients with left ventricular assist device treated with vitamin K antagonists: impact of anticoagulation quality in the randomized ARIES-HM3 trial.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: In HM3 LVAD patients on VKAs, higher time in therapeutic range (TTR) significantly reduces non-surgical bleeding, reinforcing the importance of VKA quality, with female sex and Black race identified as predictors of low TTR.

title: Treatment for Brain Metastases With Stereotactic Radiation vs Hippocampal-Avoidance Whole Brain Radiation: A Randomized Clinical Trial.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: For patients with 5-20 brain metastases, stereotactic radiation improved patient-reported symptom severity and daily functioning compared to hippocampal-avoidance whole brain radiation.

title: Clinical outcomes with oral cephalosporins as definitive treatment of Enterobacterales bacteremia from a urinary source based on cefazolin minimum inhibitory concentration.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A retrospective study suggests oral cephalosporins are effective for Enterobacterales bacteremia from a urinary source, irrespective of cefazolin MIC (≤2 mcg/mL vs 4-16 mcg/mL).

title: Functional characterization of a novel class A carbapenemase CAE-1 in carbapenem-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa clinical isolates.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A novel class A carbapenemase, CAE-1, found in carbapenem-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa, confers broad-spectrum beta-lactam resistance and is located on a mobile genetic element, necessitating enhanced surveillance.

title: Efficacy and safety of relacorilant for the treatment of patients with Cushing's syndrome (GRACE): a multicentre, phase 3, double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomised-withdrawal study.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Relacorilant, a selective glucocorticoid receptor modulator, significantly improved hypertension control in patients with Cushing's syndrome compared to placebo, supporting its use as a therapeutic option.

title: Aminoglycosides enhance meropenem/vaborbactam activity against KPC-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae in the hollow fiber infection model.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: In a hollow fiber model, aminoglycosides enhanced meropenem/vaborbactam activity against KPC-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae, especially for isolates with higher MICs, preventing resistance emergence.

title: Epidemiology and genomics of azithromycin-resistant extensively drug-resistant Salmonella enterica serovar Typhimurium in Taiwan.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Azithromycin-resistant extensively drug-resistant Salmonella Typhimurium has emerged and increased in Taiwan, evolving from MDR strains through plasmid-mediated acquisition of resistance genes, highlighting the need for genomic surveillance.

title: Comparison of Sirolimus- vs Paclitaxel-Coated Balloon Angioplasty for Femoropopliteal Artery Disease: The SIRONA Randomized Noninferiority Trial.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Sirolimus-coated balloons demonstrated noninferior clinical outcomes and comparable vascular quality of life to paclitaxel-coated balloons for femoropopliteal artery disease, offering a potential alternative.

title: Effect of Baduanjin on Blood Pressure Among Individuals With High-Normal Blood Pressure: A Multicenter, Open-Label, Blinded-Outcome Randomized Controlled Trial.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Baduanjin, a traditional Chinese exercise, significantly lowered systolic blood pressure in individuals with high-normal BP over 12 months, showing comparable efficacy to brisk walking.

title: Cerebral Venous Thrombosis in Previously Healthy Patients with Cryptococcal Meningitis.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Cerebral venous sinus thrombosis (CVST) occurs in 5.6% of immunocompetent cryptococcal meningitis patients, suggesting the infection itself, rather than thrombophilia, contributes to thrombotic risk, with MRI/MRV being valuable diagnostic tools.

title: The Gut as a Source of Infection for Fungal Pathogens: Increased Fecal Candida albicans Precedes Onset of Candida Late-Onset Sepsis in Very Preterm Infants.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Increased fecal Candida albicans and reduced bacterial diversity precede Candida late-onset sepsis in very preterm infants, suggesting the gut is a significant source of infection and a target for prevention.

title: Stepwise Evolution of Triple Antimicrobial Resistance in Neisseria meningitidis.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Invasive Neisseria meningitidis serogroup Y isolates from El Salvador were found to have concurrent resistance to penicillin, ciprofloxacin, and tetracycline, mediated by blaROB-1, gyrA T91I, and tetB, marking a critical escalation in antimicrobial resistance.

title: 2026 Acute Pulmonary Embolism Guideline-at-a-Glance.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: This entry announces the upcoming 2026 Acute Pulmonary Embolism Guideline-at-a-Glance, providing a brief overview of future recommendations.

title: Differential targeting of human pyroptotic caspase-5 and caspase-4 by Shigella OspC2 and OspC3.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: Shigella effectors OspC2 and OspC3 differentially target human caspase-5 and caspase-4 to block pyroptosis, revealing a new specificity domain and potential therapeutic targets for bacterial infection.

title: Establishment of a Cynomolgus Macaque Model for Human Adenovirus Type 55-Induced Respiratory Disease.
category: Basic science to watch (>12 months likely)

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: A new cynomolgus macaque model for human adenovirus type 55-induced respiratory disease has been established, providing a crucial platform for preclinical vaccine and therapeutic evaluation.

title: 2025 ACC/AHA/HRS/ISACHD/SCAI Guideline for the Management of Adults With Congenital Heart Disease: A Report of the American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association Joint Committee on Clinical Practice Guidelines.

llm_enrichment.one_line_summary: The 2025 ACC/AHA/HRS/ISACHD/SCAI Guideline updates recommendations for managing adults with congenital heart disease, incorporating new evidence since 2018 to guide clinical evaluation and treatment.
